item.title: Oaktree Capital-Backed ITG Raises $312.2 Million in US IPO
item.description: Digital infrastructure services firm ITG Inc. raised $312.2 million in a US initial public offering, pricing its shares below a marketed range.

item.title: Shutterstock Shares Fall After Getty Says It Will End Merger
item.description: Shares of Shutterstock Inc. plunged about 30% in after-hours trading after Getty Images Holdings Inc. said it would cancel a planned merger due to a ruling from UK regulators.

item.title: Asian Stocks Eye Gains as US Posts Stellar Quarter: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were set to rise Wednesday after US equities capped their best quarter in six years, as a rally in chipmakers and signs of economic resilience boosted optimism over corporate earnings.

item.title: Oil Steadies as Peace Talks, Flows Through Hormuz in Focus
item.description: Oil steadied after the biggest quarterly drop since the pandemic as traders monitored peace talks between the US and Iran and the return of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Alcoa Bets on Aluminum Boom With $5.6 Billion South32 Deal
item.description: Alcoa Corp. agreed to buy South32 Ltd.’s bauxite, alumina and aluminum assets in Australia, Brazil and South Africa in a deal valued at as much as $5.6 billion, cementing its position among the world’s largest aluminum producers as long-term demand strengthens.

item.title: Paris-Educated Economist Named Colombia Finance Chief: El Tiempo
item.description: Colombia’s incoming president named Miguel Gómez, a former lawmaker from a conservative political dynasty, as finance minister, according to El Tiempo newspaper.

item.title: New Zealand Should Raise Interest Rates Toward Neutral, IMF Says
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank should raise interest rates toward a neutral level this year to keep inflation risks at bay, the International Monetary Fund said in a staff report.

item.title: Trump Reports at Least $1.2 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings
item.description: President Donald Trump reported earning at least $1.2 billion in 2025 from crypto and memecoin-related businesses, according to his latest annual financial disclosure.

item.title: Debt and Risky Bets Fuel South Korea and Taiwan's AI Stock Boom
item.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, how an AI-driven stock boom in Taiwan and South Korea is enticing investors to take big risks on one of the world’s hottest market rallies.

item.title: iHerb Is Said to Select Banks for $500 Million IPO This Year
item.description: iHerb, an online retailer focused on vitamins and supplements, has picked banks to lead its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: 40年ぶり円安の先、高市首相の長期戦略、米イラン和平交渉、米経済統計、米最高裁判断

item.title: Kura Sushi to Accelerate US Growth to Fend Off Rivals’ Expansion
item.description: Conveyor-belt sushi operator Kura Sushi Inc. has an ambitious target to grow its store count by 20% a year in the US, betting that it can build market share before a wave of competitors rolls in.

item.title: Nike Declines Following Cautious Commentary on Demand
item.description: Nike Inc. reported better-than-expected results last quarter, but shares slipped on cautious commentary from executives.

item.title: US Sanctions Mexican Companies for Cartel-Linked Fuel Smuggling
item.description: The US Treasury announced sanctions against two Mexican citizens and nine companies in the country that the agency said are linked to a fuel-smuggling scheme with ties to a cartel.

item.title: Canadian Stocks Beating US for a Second Year as Banks Surge
item.description: Canada’s equity benchmark is positioned to outperform its US counterpart for a second year in a row — which would be its first back-to-back win in 15 years.

item.title: SpaceX Cuts Starlink Internet Prices in Memphis After Data Center Opposition
item.description: SpaceX is offering discounts for Starlink internet plans in Memphis, Tennessee, as the Elon Musk-led company endures blowback and legal challenges from opponents of its data centers in the area and neighboring Mississippi.

item.title: Colombia Raises Interest Rate to 12% After Court Ends Crisis
item.description: Colombia restarted interest rate rises after a court stripped the government of its power to block monetary tightening and the presidential election eased fears over central bank independence.

item.title: TikTok Finalizing Settlement of Addiction Lawsuit to Avoid Trial
item.description: TikTok is finalizing a settlement related to a lawsuit claiming that the world’s largest social media platforms are addictive to minors, according to people familiar with the matter, avoiding a jury trial scheduled for July in Los Angeles.

item.title: ECB’s Dolenc Sees No Urgency to Hike If Energy Markets Stay Calm
item.description: The European Central Bank may be able to keep interest rates unchanged at its next meeting if the situation in the Middle East doesn’t escalate in the meantime, according to Governing Council member Primoz Dolenc.

item.title: Bitcoin Slides Below $60,000 as Strategy Selloff Refuels Anxiety
item.description: Bitcoin tumbled more than 3% toward $58,000 on Tuesday as investors reversed an initial vote of confidence in Michael Saylor’s financing overhaul at Strategy Inc., raising fresh concerns that one of the cryptocurrency’s biggest buyers may no longer be a consistent source of demand.

item.title: ASML, Chip-Tool Stocks Soar as Samsung, SK Plan Investments
item.description: Shares of companies that supply semiconductor equipment are soaring this week as increased investments from leading chipmakers in South Korea bolster investors’ optimism.

item.title: Brazil Government to Start Paring Back Emergency Fuel Subsidies
item.description: Brazil will immediately start rolling back emergency fuel subsidies implemented after the start of the Middle East war as President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva seeks to show fiscal restraint, said a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Science for Automating Research
item.description: Anthropic PBC is releasing new software aimed at helping scientists automate research, in the hopes of reducing some of the tedious aspects of their work.

item.title: Spain Set to Hand Papers to Over 1 Million Undocumented Migrants
item.description: The Spanish government is set to grant residency permits to more than 1 million undocumented migrants, double the number that was projected when a mass regularization program was announced in April, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Erroneous Trades Force DTCC to Take a Rare Double-Settlement Day
item.description: The Depository Trust & Clearing Corp., which settles most trades of US securities, said it will complete two days worth of transactions on Tuesday after a significant number of erroneous trades were submitted by a member firm.

item.title: Supreme Court to Hear Apple Appeal Over Epic App Store Fees
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed to hear an appeal by Apple Inc. regarding a contempt ruling in its long-running antitrust battle with Fortnite-maker Epic Games Inc.

item.title: Schneider to Buy Industrial AI Firm Cognite for $3.1 Billion
item.description: Schneider Electric SE agreed to buy Cognite in a $3.1 billion transaction to expand its industrial data and AI software business.

item.title: Kalshi Finagled a World Cup Sponsorship Deal at a Deep Discount
item.description: When the World Cup knockout round began Sunday, Kalshi joined the likes of Coca-Cola, Visa and Adidas with sideline advertising in the stadiums — an unusual addition, given that the event is halfway over, and also that FIFA already signed up an “official prediction markets partner” in ADI Predictstreet, a startup with ties to the Abu Dhabi royal family.

item.title: BLS Should Strengthen Safeguards Around Data, Watchdog Says
item.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics made improvements to safeguard its data after several incidents in 2024 led to the early release of key economic measures, but more needs to be done to avoid future lapses, the agency’s watchdog said in a report.

item.title: EU to Allocate €3.2 Billion From New Budget for Montenegro
item.description: The European Union plans to allocate €3.2 billion ($3.7 billion) in its new seven-year budget to Montenegro once it joins the bloc by 2028 as planned.
